sourceList.title: John G. Hunt, "The Mother of Elder William Brewster of the Mayflower" in the New England Historical and Genealogical Register. Vol. 124 (Oct 1970) pages 250-254.

km.England: ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Mann於大約1490年出生在英國。他至少有2個兒子和3個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mann于大约1490年出生在英国。他至少有2个儿子和3个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: Mann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for a fierce or strong man, or for a man contrasted with a boy, from Middle English, Middle High German man, German Mann ‘man’. In some cases it may have arisen as an occupational name for a servant, from the medieval use of the term to describe a person of inferior social status. The Jewish surname can be artificial.

English and German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Manno, found in Old English as Mann or Manna. This originated either as a byname or else as a short form of a compound name containing the element man ‘man’, such as Hermann .
English: habitational name from the Isle of Man.

variants.name: Manon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Luis, Marcos, Adolfo, Alejandro, Ana, Ana Maria, Angel, Bienvenido, Cristobal, Eduardo, Ernesto, Fernando.
Galician (Mañón): variant of Maañón, a habitational name from Mañón (formerly Maañón), a place in Galicia (Spain). The surname Mañón is not found in Galicia (Spain).
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Manno (see Mann 2), with the Old French oblique case ending.

variants.name: Mannel
meanings: German (Männel): from a pet form of Mann 2.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one of the French places called Mesnil, from Old French mesnil (from Latin mansionile, a diminutive of mansio ‘abode, habitation’), used of the country domain where a noble lived with his family and retainers. As a surname it may also be used of a member of the household, retainer.
English: from the ancient Germanic female personal name Maginhild, itself from magin- ‘power’ + hild ‘battle’.

variants.name: Manns
meanings: German: patronymic from   Mann   1 and 2.
English: variant of Mann 1, 2 and 5, with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Mansell
meanings: English (mainly West Midlands):

(of Norman origin): habitational name from Old French mansel ‘inhabitant of Le Mans or the surrounding area of Maine’. The place was originally named in Latin (ad) Ceromannos, from the name of the Gaulish tribe living there, the Ceromanni. The name was shortened to Celmans and then became Le Mans as a result of the mistaken identification of the first syllable with the Old French demonstrative adjective.
status name for a particular type of feudal tenant, Anglo-Norman French mansel, one who occupied a manse (from Late Latin mansa ‘dwelling’), a measure of land sufficient to support one family.

variants.name: Mankin
meanings: English: from a pet form of the personal name Man(n) (see Mann 2), or a nickname from a diminutive of the nounman, with the sense of ‘little man’.

variants.name: Hermann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Jurgen, Mathias, Gottlieb, Horst, Juergen, Klaus, Lorenz, Bernd, Dieter, Erwin.

German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + man ‘man’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, and some other European countries; see also below. Compare Harman 5.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of German name (see 1 above). Compare   Herman  .

variants.name: Man
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wai, Chan, Cheuk, Kin, Kwok, Sai, Shek, Tong, Chi, Choong, Chung Yee, Hin.
Flemish and Dutch (also De Man): from Middle Dutch man ‘man, husband, vassal’ (compare Mann ).
English: variant of   Mann  .

variants.name: Mannes
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Aase, Erik, Lars.  German Klaus, Kurt.
German: patronymic from   Mann  .
Dutch: from the personal name Mannes, a short form of Hermannus (see Hermann ).

variants.name: Mon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jorge, Jose, Armando, Dulce, Eduardo, Emilio, Enedina, Francisca, Gregorio, Guillermina, Jose Luis, Josefa.  Chinese Chin, Hon, Hyeon, Jin, Kam Shing, Khin, Kin, Maung, Mei, Seng, Sing, Zhi Ming.
Asturian-Leonese: habitational name from Mon in Asturias (Spain).
Catalan: from a short form of the Marian name Mare de Déu del Món ‘Mother of God of the World’.

variants.name: Manheim
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Avraham, Moshe, Surie, Zalman.

Jewish (Ashkenazic) and German: habitational name from the city of Mannheim in southwestern Germany (formerly the residence of the electors Palatine), so named from the ancient Germanic personal name Manno (see Mann 2) + Old High German heim ‘homestead’. Mannheim was not fortified or chartered until the beginning of the 17th century, until which time it was just a small fishing village. This surname is rare in Germany. Compare Mannheim .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: Mann was born about 1490, in England. He had at least 2 sons and 3 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Mann est né environ 1490, à Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Mann wurde um 1490 in England geboren. Er hatte mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Mann è nato il circa 1490 nel England. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie.

ja.lifeSummary: Mannは約1490年にイギリスで生まれた。彼は少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Mann(은)는 대략 1490년 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mann nasceu aproximadamente 1490, em Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Mann родился около 1490 г. в местности Англия. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери.

es.lifeSummary: Mann nació aproximadamente 1490, en Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas.
